title: HX711 24-Bit ADC for Load Cell and Wheatstone Bridge Amplification
content: The HX711 is a 24-bit sigma-delta ADC designed for Wheatstone bridge / load cell applications. It provides a selectable gain (64 or 128 on channel A, 32 on channel B) and a 2-wire SPI-like interface (CLK + DOUT). At 80 SPS (channel A, gain 128), resolution is better than 0.1 g on a 5 kg load cell.
extended_content: Load cells produce a differential voltage of typically 1–2 mV/V full-scale from a 5 V excitation. The HX711 amplifies and digitises this signal. Calibration: apply a known weight, record raw counts, then scale_factor = raw_count / known_weight_grams. Zero offset: tare (record raw at zero load). Weight = (raw − zero_offset) / scale_factor. Common load cells: TAL220B (5 kg), CZL616C (50 kg). The HX711 AVDD can be 5 V (from MCU 5 V rail) or a separate reference. Interface: DOUT goes LOW when data is ready; MCU clocks 24 bits out; 25th pulse sets gain for next read. Library: HX711 by bogde (Arduino) or hx711 (Python/RPi). Noise reduction: shield cables; avoid mechanical vibration during read; average 4–8 readings.
units: weight: g (or kg), raw_count: 24-bit signed integer
source: Avia Semiconductor HX711 Data Sheet Rev C; bogde HX711 Arduino Library README
